channel.title: New local problems on FixMyStreet
channel.description: The latest local problems reported by users

item.title: Flytipped mattress, 24th April
item.description:
Again….

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Batley Place, Hackney, London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Pole has turned, 24th April
item.description:
When queuing in Mayplace Road West, waiting to turn left into Erith Road, it’s difficult to see when the green filter arrow comes on as the lights are not facing the right direction. It’s been like this at least a week.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 2, Mayplace Road East, Barnehurst, Bexleyheath, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet
item.category: Damage - pole leaning / equipment detached from pole/facing the wrong direction

item.title: Broken Playground Equipment which is very dangerous, 24th April
item.description:
Hi, I have visited beckneham beacon park, BR3 3QL this afternoon with my daughter who has gone to play on a piece of equipment in the baby area of the park which has harmed my daughters face. It is unsafe very wobberly has broken plastic on it , on show and is very sharp pointy and should be fixed or covered over so children can NOT play with this. I was unaware it was broken until my daughter had her accident. Which I am understanding it is an accident but would have not happened if the equipment was safe to use or covered over out of use. This needs immediate attention as this could be a lot worse next time. Thank you for your time.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 379, Croydon Road, Elmers End, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.description:
Flytipped rubbish beside waste collection chutes.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Cowley Road, Oval, Greater London

item.description:

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 44, Durham Avenue, Shortlands, Greater London

item.title: Radiator flytipped on elcot avenue, 24th April
item.description:
Please remove
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 22, Elcot Avenue, Peckham, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Deweeding on the corner of Southern Way/West Parkside, 24th April
item.description:
It’s been a full year of council ignoring its responsibilities of de-weeding this little corner between West Parkside and Southern Way. I have reported this multiple times on this platform but the posts just get deleted and no action is taken to clear up that area from weeds and rubbish. Please stop ignoring this problem, attend and de-weed the area

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Southern Way, Greenwich
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Fly tipping, 24th April
item.description:
Fly tipping

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Basingdon Way, Dulwich Village, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Greater London

item.title: Illegal flyposting on bus shelter, 24th April
item.description:
At least two adverts flyposted

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Peckham Hill Street, Peckham, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet
item.category: Graffiti / Flyposting on stop or shelter (non-offensive)

item.title: Dangerous equipment dumping to community bins, 24th April
item.description:
Dear Council Team, I would like to report an ongoing issue of illegal dumping at our communal bin area. A neighbour in our building, who appears to collect scrap materials from other people, has been repeatedly disposing of large items into the shared bins. This has been happening regularly and is causing significant problems for residents. For example, today large items including a bed and a refrigerator were left in the communal bin area. These items are not normal household waste and should not be disposed of in this way. This behaviour is: * Obstructing access to the bin area * Creating potential health and safety hazards * Leading to overflowing bins and additional waste issues for residents I kindly request that the council investigates this matter and takes appropriate action. It would also be helpful if guidance or enforcement measures could be provided to prevent further incidents.
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Upper Abbey Road, Lessness Heath, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Flyposting on bus shelter, 24th April
item.description:
Please remove, there are multiple

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 114, Peckham Hill Street, Peckham, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet
item.category: Graffiti / Flyposting on stop or shelter (non-offensive)

item.title: 3 industrial bins overflowing at 28 glenarm road block, 24th April
item.description:
Three industrial sized bins overflowing at the back of 26-30 glenarm road. Bad smell coming from them. Hasn’t been collected in a long time and unclear when it will be

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Glenarm Road, Hackney, London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: People parking in our little close and going to the gym / shops when we pay for permits to park in our own close which is fine but its being used as a free car park, 24th April
item.description:
The parking in our close is for residents only, we pay for a permit to allow us to park. We also have to pay for visitors vouchers which is absolutely fine. People use our close to park to go to the gym or the shops. It is getting to the point that during the day there is always at least one car parked that shouldnt be which we feel is unfair when we pay to park here and for our families and friends. For some reason since the begining of the year traffic wardens have stopped coming into our close so the problem is getting worse. We the residents are all either elderly, unwell or both and its unfair that we have strangers in and out of our close all day with no regard for the residents. We also have a problem with people dumping their rubbish.
Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Tom Coombs Close, Eltham
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Fly tip, 24th April
item.description:
Furniture dumped on grass in exactly the same spot as the last lot obviously the same people ??? is the camara not working overlooking the area this is happening to often 🤔

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Samuel Street, Woolwich
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Grove Vale (Corner of Copelston Road))-Flytipping, 24th April
item.description:
Trolley dumped blocking pavement and could roll into the road. Same trolley reported in the week and it has still not been removed

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 43, Grove Vale, East Dulwich, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Bed and belongings dumped, 24th April
item.description:
A divan bed base and bags of personal belongings have been on the street for a few weeks. Please can you investigate, remove and charge the landlord. Thank you
Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Timbercroft Lane, Plumstead
